titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gilbert Dean, Class of 1841, Yale College, Notes and Memorabilia
Dean (Gilbert), Class of 1841, Yale College, Notes and Memorabilia
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 23, 2023 at 09:12

did.unittitle: Gilbert Dean, Class of 1841, Yale College, notes and ephemera
did.abstract: Collection contains ephemera and notes from Gilbert Dean's (YC 1841) time at Yale. Notes appear to be primarily from classes, lectures, and society debates (some identified and dated), but also include a letter draft in which Dean reflects on "college life," poetry, a recipe, and other fragments. The pages were part of a bound volume that was repurposed as a scrapbook for clippings later in Dean's life.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gilbert Dean, Class of 1841, Yale College, notes and memorabilia (RU 1176). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 45 (YRG 45), Yale objects and memorabilia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gilbert Dean, son of Gilbert and Abigail Rogers Dean was born in Pleasant Valley, NY on August 14, 1819. Dean graduated from Yale College in 1841. He later studied law and was admitted to the bar in New York in 1844. Dean was elected as a Democrat to the 32nd and 33rd United States Congresses, and served from 1851 to 1854. He was appointed a justice to the New York Supreme Court (2nd District) in 1854, to fill the vacancy caused by the death of Seward Barculo, and remained on the bench until the end of 1855. He was a member of the New York State Assembly (15th District) in 1863 until his death in 1870.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection contains ephemera and notes from Gilbert Dean's (YC 1841) time at Yale. Notes appear to be primarily from classes, lectures, and society debates (some identified and dated), but also include a letter draft in which Dean reflects on "college life," poetry, a recipe, and other fragments. The pages were part of a bound volume that was repurposed as a scrapbook for clippings later in Dean's life.
